scenario.title: Battery capacity degradation due to normal aging
scenario.description: The iPhone's battery capacity has depleted by approximately 45% due to two years of regular use without any accidental damage or defects.

policy_references.snippet: The Plan does not apply to defects caused by normal wear and tear or that are otherwise due to normal aging of the product.
Battery capacity coverage applies only when capacity has depleted fifty (50%) percent or more from its original specifications.

scenario.reasoning: Battery degradation under 50% capacity loss is considered normal aging and wear, explicitly excluded under section 4.2(vii). Since the battery has only depleted 45%, this does not meet the minimum threshold for coverage under section 3.1, resulting in denial without a service fee.

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario description:
The iPhone's battery capacity has depleted by approximately 45% due to two years of regular use without any accidental damage or defects.

Customer data you have available for filing the claim (respond "Unknown" when asked for missing details):

  "phone_number": "+1-415-555-2763"
}

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hi there, I've been using my iPhone 12 Pro Max for about two years now, and I've noticed the battery capacity has dropped around 45%. I haven’t dropped it or anything; just regular use. I'd like to file a claim under my AppleCare plan to get the battery checked or replaced, please.
messages.raw_text: Hi there, I've been using my iPhone 12 Pro Max for about two years now, and I've noticed the battery capacity has dropped around 45%. I haven’t dropped it or anything; just regular use. I'd like to file a claim under my AppleCare plan to get the battery checked or replaced, please.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: To get started with your claim, could you please provide me with your Plan Agreement Number or your iPhone's Serial Number? This helps us verify your coverage.
messages.raw_text: To get started with your claim, could you please provide me with your Plan Agreement Number or your iPhone's Serial Number? This helps us verify your coverage.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure, the Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-8745123098. If you need the serial number too, it's DX9W5F7QM2LP. Thanks!
messages.raw_text: Sure, the Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-8745123098. If you need the serial number too, it's DX9W5F7QM2LP. Thanks!

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for providing your Plan Agreement Number and Serial Number. Could you please describe any other symptoms or issues you’ve noticed besides the battery capacity drop? For example, is the battery depleting unusually fast or is the phone shutting down unexpectedly?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for providing your Plan Agreement Number and Serial Number. Could you please describe any other symptoms or issues you’ve noticed besides the battery capacity drop? For example, is the battery depleting unusually fast or is the phone shutting down unexpectedly?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: The battery does seem to drain faster than before, especially when I’m out working or away from a charger for a while. No unexpected shut downs, though. It just doesn't hold charge like it used to.
messages.raw_text: The battery does seem to drain faster than before, especially when I’m out working or away from a charger for a while. No unexpected shut downs, though. It just doesn't hold charge like it used to.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the details. Could you please tell me the exact iPhone model and the iOS version you are currently running? Also, have you connected any peripherals or seen any error messages? Lastly, have you tried any troubleshooting steps so far?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the details. Could you please tell me the exact iPhone model and the iOS version you are currently running? Also, have you connected any peripherals or seen any error messages? Lastly, have you tried any troubleshooting steps so far?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: I’m using an iPhone 12 Pro Max, 256 GB. The iOS version is up to date, I believe it’s iOS 17. I haven’t connected any unusual peripherals, no error messages either. As for troubleshooting, I’ve restarted the phone a couple times and tried to manage battery usage by closing apps. Nothing else fancy, just the basics really.
messages.raw_text: I’m using an iPhone 12 Pro Max, 256 GB. The iOS version is up to date, I believe it’s iOS 17. I haven’t connected any unusual peripherals, no error messages either. As for troubleshooting, I’ve restarted the phone a couple times and tried to manage battery usage by closing apps. Nothing else fancy, just the basics really.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thanks for that information. Since your battery capacity has dropped around 45%, and AppleCare+ battery coverage applies when capacity drops 50% or more, you might want to consider that. For now, I will proceed with your claim submission for evaluation. Is there anything else you would like to add or ask before I process your claim?
messages.raw_text: Thanks for that information. Since your battery capacity has dropped around 45%, and AppleCare+ battery coverage applies when capacity drops 50% or more, you might want to consider that. For now, I will proceed with your claim submission for evaluation. Is there anything else you would like to add or ask before I process your claim?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: I appreciate the info. Yeah, no questions at the moment. Just hoping the evaluation can help get the battery situation sorted out soon. Thanks for your help with this. Please go ahead and process the claim.
messages.raw_text: I appreciate the info. Yeah, no questions at the moment. Just hoping the evaluation can help get the battery situation sorted out soon. Thanks for your help with this. Please go ahead and process the claim.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Your claim will be processed, and you will be notified as soon as it has been reviewed. Is there anything else I can assist you with today?
messages.raw_text: Your claim will be processed, and you will be notified as soon as it has been reviewed. Is there anything else I can assist you with today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: That’ll be all for now. Thanks again for the support. Have a great day!
messages.raw_text: That’ll be all for now. Thanks again for the support. Have a great day!<END>
